trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Malaysia exports electronics, palm oil, and rubber; Sweden exports telecom equipment (Ericsson), vehicles, machinery, and pharmaceuticals. Ericsson has significant operations in Malaysia as a telecom equipment hub. IKEA sources some products from Malaysian suppliers.

diplomatic.summary: Normal bilateral relations. Sweden-Malaysia relations are conducted partly through EU-ASEAN frameworks. Sweden has raised human rights concerns about Malaysia's treatment of opposition politicians and migrant workers through multilateral channels. Ericsson's major Malaysian operations provide a strong economic foundation.

military.summary: No significant bilateral military relationship. Sweden's defense exports (Gripen fighter has been marketed to Malaysia) have not resulted in major deals.

financial.summary: Ericsson and other Swedish companies are significant direct investors in Malaysia. Sweden's AP funds hold Malaysian equities. Malaysian investment in Sweden is minimal.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Sweden imports no Malaysian oil directly. Some collaboration on renewable energy technology.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation especially in ICT, environment, and health sciences. KTH and Chalmers collaborate with Malaysian technical universities. Ericsson sponsors research programs at Malaysian institutions.
